summary.intent: Implement the ScrollArea agnostic core component (issue #279) for the ars-ui Rust UI library — including the state machine, 8-part API, inline unit/snapshot tests, proptest, spec-conformance harness, and spec updates.
summary.outcome: PR #699 opened with a fully passing implementation (64+ lib tests, 13 snapshots, proptest, conformance, xci-fast all green). Seven rounds of Codex review addressed 24+ findings covering orientation gating, prop sync, drag clamping, timer/visibility correctness, RTL drag, and mid-drag rebase. Session ended mid-pass-7 with fixes pushed but Codex not yet re-triggered.

repo: Named-effect intent pattern: agnostic core emits Effect::AutoHide as a PendingEffect::named marker; the adapter component owns the actual timer and sends Event::HideTimeout back. Mirrors tooltip/rating_group — the spec example showing set_timeout inside the machine was incorrect.
context_changed is set whenever an apply closure runs, not on value diff — so a no-op closure still marks context changed and can cause false positives in test assertions.
ars_collections::normalize_scroll_left_rtl + RtlScrollMode is the canonical RTL scroll normalization helper; do not reinvent it in component modules.
Floating @stable toolchain (1.96.0) introduced clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else; pagination/mod.rs had a pre-existing violation that blocked any clippy run on ars-components.

insta --accept is blocked when any snapshot is unreferenced (e.g., i18n-gated highlight fixtures absent without the i18n feature); must promote pending snapshots manually via cargo insta review --accept-new.
snapshot-reviewed label must be re-applied any time a commit modifies .snap fixture files.
Local Axis{X,Y} in scroll_area differs from ars-dom's Axis{Horizontal,Vertical}; cross-crate refactor deferred as not worth the churn.
Props delay fields use plain Duration (not Option<Duration>), matching open_delay/close_delay in tooltip/hover_card/avatar — Option is reserved for 'None means off/no-dismiss'.
Proptest regression seed files written by a failing run should be reverted if the invariant causing the failure was removed.
drop(api) does not compile — Api is a borrow, not Drop; use a block scope to end the borrow instead.
cargo xci-fast is the mandatory pre-push gate (10 sub-gates: snapshot-count, spec-compile-snippets, spec-validate, clippy -D warnings, doc build, orphan-snapshots, etc.).
Codex review loop: post @codex review comment, poll for reaction lifecycle (👀 → 👍 / threads), fix each thread with TDD + spec sync, reply/resolve all threads, re-trigger.

code.finding: normalize_scroll_left_rtl(raw, content, viewport, mode: RtlScrollMode) is the canonical RTL normalization helper; both RtlScrollMode and this fn are re-exported from ars_collections root.
handle_effects calls effect.run() — the setup closure. A no_cleanup() effect schedules nothing; the adapter component must handle named effects (e.g. AutoHide) by wiring its own timer.

Pre-existing clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else violation (map_or_else(.., identity) → unwrap_or_else(..)) that now fires under stable 1.96.0; fixed as a zero-risk one-liner to unblock clippy on the crate.

is_session_active() must include ThumbDragging so resize mid-drag keeps the scrollbar visible. MouseEnter and hover-leave paths must also guard against an active drag to avoid aborting captured-pointer interactions.

update_visibility(active: bool) — the active flag must reflect whether the resulting state is Hovering or ScrollActive. Callers: init(false), Resize(is_session_active), SyncProps(active after state resolution). Passing a hardcoded value caused stale visibility on resize and mode-change.

ThumbDragStart must cancel_effect(AutoHide) to prevent a stale timer from hiding mid-drag. ThumbDragEnd in ScrollActive must re-emit AutoHide to restart the timer. SyncProps switching away from Scroll must also cancel AutoHide.
Horizontal thumb drag in RTL must invert the delta (delta_x = -delta_x) before computing new thumb position, because RTL scroll offset increases leftward.
Mid-drag resize must rebase drag_start_thumb_pos by (thumb_new - thumb_old) for the current scroll offset, preserving the grab offset so the next move event stays continuous.

CornerSquare part needs data-ars-visible (both axes visible = true). Scrollbar track/thumb parts should use data-ars-orientation for styling — NOT aria-orientation, which is invalid on role=none elements.

ScrollArea proptest invariants: (1) orientation gating — Vertical never sets scrollbar_x_visible, Horizontal never sets scrollbar_y_visible; (2) offsets always in [0, max_scroll] after any event sequence.

workflow: Post-implementation audit is a mandatory 3-phase step: (1) spec/impl drift comparison, (2) adjacent surfaces (adapter specs, manifest, catalog, CI gates), (3) llvm-cov coverage audit with justified-miss documentation.

Each Codex review pass: fetch unresolved threads via gh api, fix each with a failing test first, sync the spec, run xci-fast, commit, push, reply to each thread with commit + test names, resolve all, re-trigger @codex review.
When a Codex finding challenges a design decision (not a clear bug), surface the tradeoff to the user before implementing to avoid reverting sound architectural choices.
Insta snapshot rename requires deleting the old .snap file and regenerating; just renaming the test leaves an orphan that blocks the orphan-check gate.
Spec code blocks that are adapter-sketch fragments (not runnable Rust) need rust,no_check fences to pass the spec-compile-snippets gate.
The proptest Event arbitrary must include all newly added events (e.g. SyncProps) to exercise the full state space.

friction: Rust closure type inference required explicit `&mut Context` annotations on all context_only closures — compiler errors were not localized to the offending closure.
insta --accept blocked by pre-existing i18n-gated unreferenced snapshots; required manual cargo insta review --accept-new to promote only new fixtures.

Seven rounds of Codex review (24+ findings) with each round surfacing deeper edge cases in the ThumbDragging/visibility/timer interaction space — significant iteration overhead for a state-machine-heavy component.
Session limit hit mid-session requiring the user to issue 'Continue' to resume.
git push did not complete atomically with the commit; required a separate push verification step.
Pre-existing clippy error in pagination/mod.rs blocked all clippy runs on ars-components until fixed, even though it was unrelated to the task.

The spec's platform-timer auto-hide example was misleading — it showed set_timeout inside the agnostic machine, contradicting the codebase's adapter-owned-timer convention; required investigation of tooltip/rating_group patterns to identify the correct approach.
open_items: PR #699 awaiting Codex pass-7 re-trigger and final 👍 before merge; pen/pel/pep fixes were pushed but @codex review was not yet re-posted at transcript end.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter components for ScrollArea are out of scope for this task (separate future issues).

AutoHide timer wiring in adapter components: core emits Effect::AutoHide as a named marker; adapter must start/restart/cancel the timer and send Event::HideTimeout back — documented in spec §1.6.1 but not yet implemented.
Foundation promotion of local Axis{X,Y} vs ars-dom's Axis{Horizontal,Vertical} deferred as a separate cross-cutting refactor.
Issue #279 board status still In Progress — will auto-close to Done via 'Closes #279' on PR merge.
